matches.evidence: forEach with async callbacks doesn't await anything. These promises are floating. If any of them fail, you'll get unhandled promise rejections... Now the DB operations are inside the fire-and-forget too, so you can silently fail to clean up reminder records. This pattern appears in multiple places across the diff — handleCancelBooking.ts, bookings.tsx, workflows.tsx. None of the forEach loops await the delete calls.
matches.reasoning: The review explicitly identifies the same root cause: deleteScheduledEmailReminder and deleteScheduledSMSReminder called without await inside forEach loops, meaning reminder cancellations are fire-and-forget and may silently fail.

matches.evidence: Why does cancelling an email with `immediateDelete: true` skip the DB deletion? That's a leaked record waiting to happen... Reminders cancelled with `immediateDelete: true` from workflow/step deletion will have their SendGrid batch cancelled but the `WorkflowReminder` row will persist forever.
matches.reasoning: The review explicitly identifies the same defect: the immediateDelete:true path in deleteScheduledEmailReminder cancels SendGrid but never deletes the WorkflowReminder DB record, creating orphaned rows.
